title: Options: A call option gives the holder the right, but not...
summary: Distinguish between the right granted by options and the obligation in futures contracts.
prompt: A call option gives the holder the right, but not the obligation, to buy an asset at a specified price by a certain date.
solution: This statement is true as it defines the fundamental characteristic of a call option. A call option grants the holder the right, but not the obligation, to purchase a specific underlying asset at a predetermined strike price before or at the expiration date. This is a core concept in options trading and is discussed in the introductory sections of the materials provided, particularly in the context of the definitions of options.
